diagram:
SELECT ... FROM Orders o JOIN OrderItems i ON o.OrderID = i.OrderID

   o (few rows)
       |
       v
 +---------------+
 | NESTED LOOP ? |
 +---------------+
       ^
       |
 i via index(OrderID)
prompt: For a query joining Orders (small filtered set) to OrderItems (indexed by OrderID), which join operator is usually preferred?

feedback.correct: Correct! Nested Loop Join is typically efficient when the outer set is small and the inner side can be searched via index.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Re-check the conditions: small outer set + index on join key usually favors Nested Loop Join.
